Connect to Baserow using either a Databa: Connect to Baserow using either a Database Token (recommended) or your Email & Password. Fill in only the fields for your chosen method — leave the other section blank.
Choose **Database Token** (recommended): Choose **Database Token** (recommended) for scoped, per-table access compatible with 2FA. Choose **Email & Password** only if you need automatic webhook registration on triggers — 2FA accounts are not supported.

#### 🔑 Database Token
Go to **Settings → Database tokens** in Baserow, create a token, copy it, and paste it below. Leave Email and Password blank.
Your Baserow database token. Leave blank: Your Baserow database token. Leave blank when using Email & Password.

#### 👤 Email & Password (JWT)
Enter your Baserow login credentials below. Leave Database Token blank. Accounts with 2FA enabled are not supported.
Your Baserow account email. Leave blank: Your Baserow account email. Leave blank when using Database Token.
Your Baserow account password. Leave bla: Your Baserow account password. Leave blank when using Database Token.
Creates a new row in a table.
When enabled, single/multi-select values: When enabled, single/multi-select values that do not yet exist in the field will be added before creating the row. Existing options are preserved.
When enabled, single/multi-select values that do not yet exist in the field will be added before updating the row. Existing options are preserved.
When enabled, single/multi-select values that do not yet exist in the field will be added before creating or updating the row. Existing options are preserved.
Gets a single row by its ID from a table: Gets a single row by its ID from a table.
Lists rows from a table with optional se: Lists rows from a table with optional search, sorting, and filtering.
Page number to return. Defaults to 1.
Number of rows to return per page. Maxim: Number of rows to return per page. Maximum 200. Defaults to 100.
Return only rows whose cell data matches: Return only rows whose cell data matches this search term.
Field name to sort by. Prefix with **-**: Field name to sort by. Prefix with **-** for descending or **+** for ascending. Example: `-Name` sorts by Name Z→A.
How to combine multiple filters. **AND**: How to combine multiple filters. **AND** requires all filters to match; **OR** requires any one filter to match.
**How to add filters** (optional):\n\nEa:
**How to add filters** (optional):

Each filter is a JSON object with three keys:
- `field` — numeric field ID (in Baserow, click the field header; the ID appears in the page URL)
- `type` — operator: `equal`, `not_equal`, `contains`, `contains_not`, `higher_than`, `lower_than`, `is_empty`, `is_not_empty`
- `value` — the value to compare against

Example: `{"field": 123, "type": "equal", "value": "Active"}`
Each entry is a JSON object with \"field: Each entry is a JSON object with "field" (numeric ID), "type" (operator), and "value". Leave empty to return all rows.
Finds a row by matching a field value. R: Finds a row by matching a field value. Returns the first match.
Select the field to search by.
The value to search for (exact match).
Updates fields in an existing row. Empty: Updates fields in an existing row. Empty values are skipped. To clear a field, use Clean Row.
Creates a new row or updates an existing: Creates a new row or updates an existing one by matching a field value.
Select the field to search for an existi: Select the field to search for an existing row.
The value to search for (exact match). I: The value to search for (exact match). If a row with this value is found it will be updated; otherwise a new row is created.
Sets all fields in a row to empty/null. To update only specific fields, use Update Row instead.
Creates multiple rows in a single reques: Creates multiple rows in a single request. Accepts up to 200 rows.
A JSON array of objects. Each object rep: A JSON array of objects. Each object represents a row with field names as keys.
Updates multiple rows in a single reques: Updates multiple rows in a single request. Each row must include an "id" field. Accepts up to 200 rows.
A JSON array of objects. Each object mus: A JSON array of objects. Each object must include an "id" field and the fields to update.
Deletes multiple rows in a single reques: Deletes multiple rows in a single request. Accepts up to 200 row IDs.
Numeric IDs of the rows to delete. You c: Numeric IDs of the rows to delete. You can get row IDs from the List Rows or Find Row actions.
Calculates an aggregation (sum, average,: Calculates an aggregation (sum, average, min, max, count, etc.) over all values of a field in a grid view.
Select the grid view to aggregate.
The calculation to run over the field. *: The calculation to run over the field. **Sum, Average, Min, Max, Median, Std Dev, and Variance** only work with number fields.
Uploads a file to Baserow from a URL. Re: Uploads a file to Baserow from a URL. Returns the uploaded file object that can be used in file fields. Requires Email & Password (JWT) authentication — Database Tokens do not have access to the user-files endpoint.
The public URL of the file to upload to: The public URL of the file to upload to Baserow.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
HEAD: HEAD
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
Triggers when a new row is created in a: Triggers when a new row is created in a Baserow table.
Triggers when an existing row is updated: Triggers when an existing row is updated in a Baserow table.
Triggers when a row is deleted from a Ba: Triggers when a row is deleted from a Baserow table.
Triggers when a row is created, updated,: Triggers when a row is created, updated, or deleted in a Baserow table. To react to only one event type, use the dedicated New Row, Updated Row, or Deleted Row triggers.
Triggers when new rows are created in a: Triggers when new rows are created in a Baserow table. Returns all rows from the event as a single batch.
Triggers when existing rows are updated: Triggers when existing rows are updated in a Baserow table. Returns all rows from the event as a single batch.
Triggers when rows are deleted from a Ba: Triggers when rows are deleted from a Baserow table. Returns all deleted row IDs from the event as a single batch.
✅ **Webhook auto-registered** — no manua: ✅ **Webhook auto-registered** — no manual setup needed. The webhook is created and removed automatically when you enable or disable this trigger.
**Manual webhook setup required** (Datab:
**Manual webhook setup required** (Database Token auth):

1. In Baserow, click the **···** menu beside your table and select **Webhooks**.
2. Click **Create webhook +**.
3. Set the HTTP method to **POST**.
4. Paste this URL into the endpoint field:
```
{{webhookUrl}}
```
5. Under **Events**, select **Rows created**.
6. Click **Save**.

**Manual webhook setup required** (Database Token auth):

1. In Baserow, click the **···** menu beside your table and select **Webhooks**.
2. Click **Create webhook +**.

5. Under **Events**, select **Rows created, Rows updated, and Rows deleted**.
